body:
Completes the RRTMG conceptual redesign: a one-line path to run RRTMGP without a host model, for teaching and single-column experiments. Additive; no changes to the existing solver or getter API.

**`prepare_atmosphere!(solver)`** exposes the input-preparation cascade (level interpolation → isothermal boundary layer → clipping → column amounts) as a callable step, so hosts can inspect the prepared state without solving. `update_fluxes!` is now literally `prepare_atmosphere!` followed by the solve/combine steps — the prepare/solve split familiar from other radiation drivers.

**`standard_atmosphere(FT; kind)`** builds an idealized clear-sky `AtmosphereProfile` (`:tropical`, `:midlatitude_summer`, `:subarctic_winter`) — analytic two-segment temperature, exact hydrostatic pressure, idealized water vapor/ozone, present-day well-mixed gases.

**`solve(profile; method)`** solves it in one call, returning a documented **`RadiationOutput`** struct with stable field names (`lw_up` … `sw_direct_dn`, `net`, `heating_rate`, `solver`). Default `ClearSkyRadiation` (needs lookup tables); `GrayRadiation` runs after a bare `using RRTMGP` with no NetCDF. `solve_gray` now returns the same struct (its old NamedTuple used identical names, so access is unchanged).

```julia
using RRTMGP, NCDatasets
out = RRTMGP.solve(RRTMGP.standard_atmosphere(Float64; kind = :tropical))
out.net; out.heating_rate
```

**Docs**: new "Fortran and paper concordance" page mapping RRTMGP.jl names → reference `rte-rrtmgp` `mo_*`/`ty_*` modules and kernels → the papers whose equations they implement, so Fortran-literate readers can navigate the code.

Stacked on #606 (`ts/kernel-refactor`).
title: Standalone front door + Fortran concordance docs (#4)

body:
Brings the standalone / single-column ("Layer-3") RRTMGP work onto `main`. This is the content that was merged into `ts/kernel-refactor` via #604; here it is retargeted at `main` as a clean, linear branch (`main` is a direct ancestor of the tip, so there is no merge commit and no conflicts).

## What's included (5 commits)

- **`prepare_atmosphere!`** — the prepare/solve split, so hosts can inspect the prepared atmospheric state without solving (W5, part 1).
- **`standard_atmosphere`, `solve(profile)`, `RadiationOutput`** — the standalone single-column entry points that need no ClimaCore (W5, part 2).
- **Fortran-and-paper concordance docs page** — maps RRTMGP.jl names to the RTE-RRTMGP `mo_*`/`ty_*` names and the paper equations.
- **Code deduplication and robustness increases** — shared `compute_gas_optics_core`, stored per-band `flux_net`, shortwave two-stream resonance/energy-conservation robustness.
- **Lookup-sourced temperature clip bounds** — read the clip range from the lookup tables' reference temperatures (`t_ref_min`/`t_ref_max`) instead of ClimaParams, and remove the now-unused `optics_lookup_temperature_min/max` parameters.

## Notes

- Docbuild-critical fixes are already in the content: the `compute_gas_optics` docstring is on the public method and `get_t_min` is listed in `docs/src/api.md`, so the docs `missing_docs`/`docs_block` checks pass.
- One intermediate commit message has a typo ("tempe**Get**" → "temperature"); harmless if this PR is squash-merged.
title: Standalone single-column RRTMGP path (prepare/solve split, standard_atmosphere, RadiationOutput)
